title: Salesforce Permission Set Escalation Detection
dataSources: `index=salesforce` sourced from **`salesforce:event_log`**: Salesforce **ELF** records via **Event Log File REST API**, default **3600s polling** on the TA modular input (**Event Monitoring add-on / Salesforce Shield** licensing required). This UC depends on **`EventType="SetupAuditTrail"`** rows with actions such as **`assignPermSetAction`** and **`insertPermission`**. Key extracted fields typically include **`PermissionSet`** (API name/label), **`DelegateUser`** (admin performing change), **`UserId`** / **`Display`** (grantee), **`Section`**, **`Action`**, and timestamps—validate against TA `props.conf` after upgrades because SetupAuditTrail schemas evolve. **Optional:** mirror **Login History** inputs to detect concurrent admin sessions—not required for baseline. **Data flow:** Salesforce admin audit stream → collector → Splunk index. **CIM note:** No **native CIM** mapping; treat as proprietary JSON and align to **IGA** ticketing via lookups rather than accelerated models.
app: `Splunk Add-on for Salesforce` ([Splunkbase 3549](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/3549)), ≥5.0. Install on the Search Head (for field extractions and saved searches) and on a dedicated Heavy Forwarder if running data collection off-SH. The TA provides both collection and parsing. The TA requires a Salesforce Connected App with OAuth 2.0 credentials and an Event Monitoring or Salesforce Shield license.
spl:
index=salesforce sourcetype="salesforce:event_log" EventType="SetupAuditTrail" Action IN ("assignPermSetAction","insertPermission")
| search PermissionSet IN ("*ModifyAllData*","*ViewAllData*","*ManageUsers*","*CustomizeApplication*")
| stats values(DelegateUser) as granted_by, values(PermissionSet) as perms, count by UserId, Display
| sort -count
description: Detects grants of high-impact permission sets—including broad data visibility, user administration, and application customization—from Setup Audit Trail assignments and permission inserts that fall outside predictable IT service windows.
value: Unchecked privilege expansion invites insider sabotage and regulator findings that segregation-of-duties failed; documenting and reversing toxic combinations quickly reduces breach blast radius and supports clean SOX, privacy, and financial crime control narratives.
implementation: Stream Setup Audit Trail via Event Monitoring; maintain a curated list of toxic permission keywords; correlate DelegateUser identities with ticketing or change CAB IDs; escalate after-hours grants automatically.
visualization: **Row 1 —** Timeline (**`span=15m`**, default **Last 7 days** zoom=24h) of toxic permission events with color by **`Action`**; drilldown sets tokens for **`UserId`**. **Row 2 —** Donut summarizing **`DelegateUser`** frequency to detect habitual over-assigners vs one-offs. **Row 3 —** Table: **`Display`, `UserId`, `perms`, `granted_by`, `count`, `_time`** with export to SOC workbook. **Row 4 —** Linked **SOC ticket panel** listing open incidents keyed on `UserId` via REST or lookup (Splunk OPS queue) showing analyst owner and SLA timer.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable for the primary search: it relies on application- and industry-specific log formats, not a normalized CIM data model.

references.title: Splunk Add-on for Salesforce — Data Collection Configuration

knownFalsePositives: (a) **Emergency break-glass / Sev0 incidents:** privileged grants like **Modify All Data** legitimately occur during outages—**mitigate** mandatory `break_glass` CAB/RFC correlation, automatic expiry workflows within two hours, and paging only when no ticket linkage exists after grace period. (b) **Quarterly IAM recertification & role harmonization:** bulk **assignPermSetAction** bursts replay hundreds of rows—**mitigate** ingest `iga_campaign` spreadsheets into `iga_campaign_window` lookups with blackout windows narrower than naive full suppression to preserve drift detection at campaign edges. (c) **Managed-package provisioning:** AppExchange installs push template permission sets labelled similarly to toxic patterns—**mitigate** whitelist `PackageNamespace`, tag `DelegateUser` integration bots, and escalate only when namespace unknown. (d) **Partner SI engagements:** consultants temporarily receive powerful sets per SOW—**mitigate** timed `partner_whitelist` with contracting officer approval IDs and correlated VPN posture where available. (e) **Sandbox / scratch-org harmonization ahead of prod releases:** repeated assignments mirror attacks—**mitigate** exclude non-production org IDs from prod alerts while still monitoring sandboxes separately for insider labs. (f) **Post-M&A profile merges:** identity merges create noisy privilege churn—**mitigate** executive-comms macro, HR effective-date joins, and forensic sampling instead of blind throttling.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• **`Splunk Add-on for Salesforce` ≥5.0** on **Search Heads** (field extractions, saved searches, dashboards) and, for production hygiene, a **Heavy Forwarder** that performs OAuth-authenticated REST collection away from interactive admin sessions.
• **Salesforce entitlements & app security:** **Event Monitoring add-on** and/or **Salesforce Shield** for **Event Log File (ELF) REST** access; **Connected App** with **OAuth 2.0**; least-privilege integration user; credentials in Splunk **storage/passwords** or your secret manager—not cleartext in `local/inputs.conf`.
• **Indexes & RBAC:** route to **`index=salesforce`** (or `salesforce_prod` vs `salesforce_sb` macros); SOC and IAM analysts get **`srchIndexesAllowed`** without `admin`; restrict dashboard tokens exposing `Username`/`UserId` broadly.
• **Ingest economics:** default ELF poll **3600s**; pilot 7–14 days and forecast **GB/day**; trim unnecessary EventTypes if license pressure emerges; document `_indextime` vs `_time` skew monitoring for catch-up bursts.
• **Lookups & governance:** provision change-controlled CSV/KV lookups (allowlists, object classification tiers, integration registries); define **RACI** across CRM platform engineering, SOC, IAM, Data Owners, and Legal for escalations touching regulated datasets.
• **Extraction regression guard:** after each TA upgrade, run `fieldsummary` on the UC’s canonical fields for 10k recent events and archive results in Git to catch JSON path drift early.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Enable `salesforce:event_log` with SetupAuditTrail coverage. Key fields: EventType, Action, PermissionSet, DelegateUser, UserId, Display, Section, _time. Validate field extractions for permission set API names vs. labels; expand `PermissionSet` wildcard list to match your org naming standards.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Use a short lookback (15–60 minutes) with summary indexing for history if needed:

```spl
index=salesforce sourcetype="salesforce:event_log" EventType="SetupAuditTrail" Action IN ("assignPermSetAction","insertPermission")
| search PermissionSet IN ("*ModifyAllData*","*ViewAllData*","*ManageUsers*","*CustomizeApplication*")
| stats values(DelegateUser) as granted_by, values(PermissionSet) as perms, count by UserId, Display
| sort -count
```

Understanding this SPL
SetupAuditTrail rows with assign or insert actions are narrowed to dangerous permission labels using subsearch wildcards, then aggregated to show who received power, who delegated it, and how often duplicates fire—useful for spotting repeated break-glass abuse or automation runaway.

Step 3 — Validate
Cross-check each hit in Salesforce Setup → Permission Set Assignments and change records. Confirm DelegateUser matches an active administrator; escalate if contractors or orphaned integrations appear. Retrofit alerting with allowed maintenance windows using `business_hours` lookups.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Open incidents with entitlement snapshots, integrate with privileged access governance workflows, and feed a compliance dashboard illustrating mean time to revoke toxic sets after alert receipt.

Permission set escalation — advanced interpretation and SoD alignment
**SetupAuditTrail** entries are the evidentiary backbone for privilege changes, but field semantics differ across Salesforce editions and admin experiences. Build a **dictionary** mapping raw `PermissionSet` API names to **business capabilities** (for example, `Custom_Permission_Set_Finance_Admin` → “Modify All on Financial Objects”). When wildcards like `*ModifyAllData*` are used, reconcile periodically with Salesforce **Permission Set Groups** refactor projects that rename artifacts—stale patterns cause silent false negatives.

Segregation of Duties overlays
Construct a KV store **`sod_pairs.csv`** forbidding simultaneous assignment of conflicting sets (payments + vendor master maintenance). Extend the SPL using `transaction` or **`stats latest()` windowing** to detect **`pair_violation` within rolling 24h**, not solely isolated grants. Coordinate with IAM recert campaigns to avoid alert storms during bulk cleanup—import expected batch IDs.

Privileged access workstation context
Cross-check **DelegateUser** with **PAW enrollment** inventories; grants from unmanaged workstations escalate severity. Integration with endpoint tools (CrowdStrike, Intune compliance) strengthens narrative.

Delegation chain analytics
Analyze repeated grants from a single **DelegateUser** using **`streamstats` count by DelegateUser`** to spotlight **delegation burnout** patterns or compromised admin credentials.

Emergency access integration
Formal **break-glass** accounts must emit predetermined **RFC codes** annotated in ticketing; lookups match codes to Splunk-side exemption rules (lookup-driven) before paging.

Visualization of blast radius approximation
Leverage **`values(PermissionSet)`** cardinalities correlated with **`UserRole`** lookups approximating reachable object counts—not perfect but helpful triage prioritization.

SPL variant using subsearch exclusions
Maintain `known_low_risk_patterns` minimizing noise from scripted harmonization bots.

Historical reconstructions
Dump monthly snapshots into summary index preserving **grant deltas** enabling **retroactive entitlement reviews** mandated by regulators.

Forensic duplication prevention
Ensure multiple alerts for same logical grant collapse via **`dedup`** keyed on hash of `{UserId, PermissionSet,_timerounded}` rounded to nearest 5 minutes to handle duplicate ELF deliveries.

Change management interplay
Mandatory `change_ticket` enrichment field—if absent, auto-severity uplift.

Architectural redundancy
Duplicate alert to **Slack CAB channel** concurrently with SOC for realtime visibility during maintenance moratoriums.

Behavioral anomaly: off-hours clustering
Histogram **hour-of-day** for delegate actions; outliers highlight potential offshore compromise.

Quarterly KPI set
Mean time to revoke toxic sets once detected, recurrence rate per admin, backlog age for outstanding investigations tied to privileged grants.

Purple team playbook
Controlled assignment of benign toxic-sounding sandbox permission set validated end-to-end.

Training for CRM admins
Emphasizes least privilege when cloning profiles during project sprints—common root cause bursts.

Evidence standards
Retain Splunk dashboards plus Salesforce **permission set audit** PDF exports zipped with manifest.

Third-party admins
Partners flagged via `consulting_firm_netblocks` aligning IP context when available indirectly through IdP correlation.

Rollback automation hooks
Orchestration to **bulk remove assignments** scripted but gated behind dual approvals—document linkage to Splunk-derived incident IDs only as references, execution remains in Salesforce tooling.

Psychological nuance during investigations
Maintain empathy protocols when investigating insiders—coordinate with HR before confrontational outreach.

Executive summary KPI
Count of unresolved **tier-1 privilege** anomalies >48h aging.

Splunk RBAC segregation
Separate roles for entitlement viewers vs investigative deep divers.

Indexing hot/warm tiers
Privileged audit events may merit longer hot retention due to investigative frequency.

Correlation with CSPM irrelevant but note SaaS CASB parity for completeness mindset.

FAQ for auditors
Demonstrate deterministic matching logic for wildcard permission patterns and quarterly reviews.

Adaptive learning
Use analyst labels to shrink generic wildcards into explicit enumerations safely over time.

API limits during polling spikes
Privileged changes burst after release nights—coordinate poll intervals minimally.

Regional compliance
EU vs US admin bases segmented monitoring windows respecting local labor directives.

Incident closure taxonomy
Buckets: benign change, tooling artifact, insider policy breach, compromised admin creds.

Retroactive alerting after pattern updates
Historical scheduled backfill playbook ensures previous latent toxic combos surface once pattern knowledge improves—run in controlled outage windows due to expense.

Quarterly tabletop integration
Finance + IAM + SOC joint scenario using synthetic toxic assignment.

Splunk Observability linkage
Low priority but optionally overlay **Synthetic checks** verifying admin login surfaces availability—prevent false negatives when admin lockouts block legitimate revocation actions.

Governance escalation
Unresolved toxic grants > SLA automatically notify CISO delegate.

Technical debt tracking
Maintain backlog of brittle wildcards slated for refactoring.

Environmental drift
Sandbox noise excluded via org ID macros—reassert monthly.
Operational posters optional—cultural reinforcement.

Collaboration KPI
Cross-functional meeting attendance rate quarterly.

Psychological metrics
Analyst burnout watch—rotate hot alerts.
Longitudinal trending shows improvement post training—report annually.

Splunk Phantom/SOAR (if present)
Embed playbooks querying Splunk for context then opening Salesforce cases—document API service user least privilege.

Edge case: permission set group inheritance
When SF introduces nested structures, expand monitoring to include **group assignment** events—validate TA coverage by sampling JSON.

Edge case: automated CI/CD metadata deploys
Jenkins pushing metadata may log as system user—classify via `DelegateUser` mapping.

Edge case: partial log truncation
If events arrive truncated, rely on secondary cross-check with weekly Salesforce **full export** entitlement reports not for automation but assurance sampling.

Edge case: multilingual labels
Wildcards on English substrings may miss localized permission labels—extend patterns carefully with governance.

Edge case: unicode display names
Normalize with `iconv` assumptions cautiously—prefer API names over labels in logic where feasible.

Edge case: duplicate UserId merges
Post-M&A identity merges may produce confusing chronology—join HR effective dates.

Edge case: delayed DelegateUser backfill
Rare ingest ordering issues—use `transaction` maxspan if needed.

Edge case: multi-org aggregation
Ensure `OrganizationId` tokenized macros filter correctly.

Edge case: API user acting as delegate
Flag differently—service accounts lack human intent nuance.

Edge case: seasonal hiring spikes
Onboarding drives bulk grants—calendar alignment.

Edge case: audit logging disabled briefly
Catastrophic control failure—separate health monitor on event volume floor.

Edge case: encryption at rest irrelevant for Splunk side but note classification of stored JSON.

Edge case: cross-cloud forwarder misrouting
Validate `_indextime` vs `_time` skew patterns.

Quarterly KPI set (additive)
Median assignment count per admin per month for workload balancing insights.

Executive education microsession
Quarterly 15-minute briefing for CIO staff.

Research horizon
Evaluate streaming near-real-time event monitoring deltas if budgets allow migrating highest-risk subsets.

Psychological closure
Celebrate clean quarters with benign metrics storytelling.
Monthly blog internally summarizing trending permission categories changed.

FAQ customer-facing sanitization guidance
Marketing must not accidentally leak investigation existence—coordinate messaging.

Historical archive policy
Cold storage rotations documented.

Quarterly KPI set recap
Reuse consistent definitions across all Salesforce security UCs for portfolio reporting.

Portfolio cross-link UC-10.12.46/47
Escalations may chain—maintain macros referencing correlated user risk.
Tune searches to minimize carbon footprint via reduced scan—it matters to some regulated banks' ESG narratives symbolically—optional mention.
Realtime detection with deterministic policy engine—Splunk complements.

Appendix numbering discipline
Maintain versioned runbook PDFs aligning to Splunk exported SPL revision tags.

Quarterly KPI set normalization
Normalize per 10k Salesforce licenses for benchmarking across subsidiaries.

Incident simulation drift detection
Annual re-simulate foundational scenarios ensuring knowledge retention.
Maintain professional tone.

Governance, evidence, and continuous control testing
Establish this detection as a named IT General Control for custodial CRM data. Each quarter, export a statistically significant sample of alerts (for example, 25 cases) and perform a **quality review** covering five dimensions: (1) timeliness relative to Salesforce poll latency, (2) accuracy versus ground truth in Salesforce admin screens, (3) completeness of ticketing metadata, (4) appropriateness of suppressions, and (5) analyst narrative quality suitable for regulatory interviews. Store evidence in your GRC repository with hashes and retention aligned to your legal hold policy. When Splunk or the Salesforce TA upgrades, repeat a **regression harness** that replays frozen JSON fixtures through a dev indexer to ensure field extractions remain stable. Maintain a **change advisory** record whenever thresholds, lookup schemas, or cron schedules change, because auditors will ask for a traceable history of tuning decisions.

Performance, workload, and search optimization
Salesforce security searches can become expensive if they scan wide time ranges without partition discipline. Prefer **index-time routing** that isolates production org data, and use **`tstats` only when you have a compatible data model**—which is rarely true for raw Salesforce JSON—so focus on **fast filters** (`sourcetype`, `EventType`, `index`) and **summary indexing** for baselines. On Splunk Cloud, monitor **search budget** consumption for the service account running these schedules; split heavy baseline jobs from alert jobs. For on-premises, place collection on a **Heavy Forwarder** to keep REST polling off search heads. If you enable **workload management**, assign these searches to a pool with guardrails so ad-hoc human searches do not starve operational detections.

Risk communication and executive narrative
Translate technical hits into business language. For each incident class, maintain a one-paragraph **executive summary template** describing customer impact, financial exposure, and remediation status. This elevates the UC from a SOC tool to a board-ready control narrative, especially in financial services where CRM data includes wealth, insurance, or mortgage artifacts. Pair metrics with **leading indicators** (integration onboarding rate, active connected apps) so risk committees can ask forward-looking questions rather than only reviewing rear-view alert counts.

Deep-dive operational walkthrough — toxic permission changes with SoD rigor
Begin every investigation by freezing a **point-in-time narrative**: who received access, who delegated it, what exact permission labels were involved, and whether the event occurred during a known change freeze. In financial services, **Segregation of Duties (SoD)** narratives often matter more than the raw privilege string: an analyst should map each permission set to **business capabilities** using your internal dictionary before judging severity. If your dictionary is incomplete, treat unknown labels as **high uncertainty** and escalate to the Salesforce Center of Excellence for classification within the SLA defined by your risk appetite.

When `DelegateUser` is a service account, pivot immediately to **CI/CD** and **release management** records. Many “toxic” grants are actually automated metadata deployments triggered by legitimate pipelines but logged impersonating a bot principal. Conversely, when `DelegateUser` is a human administrator, verify **PAW compliance**, **MFA strength**, and whether the admin’s session originated from an anomalous region using UC‑10.12.49 techniques in a secondary saved search macro.

For break-glass scenarios, require a **pre-registered incident command ticket** with an approved **RFC** or **CAB** exception code. If no code is present, auto-escalate severity and page the **Salesforce platform duty manager** even if the permission change appears business-justified verbally—post-incident, patch process gaps rather than lowering alert sensitivity.

Investigations should produce two artifacts: (1) a **Splunk evidence snapshot** (dashboard PDF with SPL appendix) and (2) a **Salesforce-native entitlement report** pulled through approved admin procedures. The second artifact is essential because auditors often prefer platform-native evidence for SOX testing, while Splunk evidence demonstrates **continuous monitoring** control operation.

During mergers, expect **permission harmonization waves** that look like attacks. Mitigate with **M&A calendars** and **temporary composite suppressions** tied to explicit integration milestones, not open-ended whitelist entries. Each suppression carries an **expiration** and responsible executive.

Maintain a quarterly **permissions risk committee** briefing summarizing counts of toxic grants, mean time to revoke, and anecdotes of narrowly avoided insider incidents surfaced by proactive monitoring—not just alert volume—to sustain funding for governance staffing.

Purple team validations should include benign toxic-sounding sandbox permission assignments executed under change control with measured Splunk latency and analyst comprehension checks (can a junior analyst explain why the benign event is benign in under five minutes?). If comprehension fails, improve dashboards and field formatting before expanding scope.

Close the loop by periodically sampling **negative space**: high-risk users who generated **no alerts** despite active admin engagements—sometimes false negatives arise from malformed wildcards—use these samples to refine detection patterns ethically without turning monitoring into pervasive surveillance theater.
Schedule as Alert: cron **`*/30 * * * *`** given short lookback semantics for SetupAuditTrail, time range **`-45m@m` to `now`**, trigger **results > 0**, **throttle** `UserId + PermissionSet` **12h**, auto-severity escalation when `DelegateUser` not in `sf_privileged_operator` cohort or `_time` outside `business_hours_macro`.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **Symptom — Wildcard permission patterns miss renamed sets** — Salesforce relabels during permission-set-group migrations; refresh wildcards monthly and maintain explicit API name table.  
• **Symptom — Duplicate rows for same assignment** — duplicate ELF retrieval or replicated inputs; inspect `splunk btool inputs` on collectors and dedup using `eval sig=UserId.PermissionSet.time_bucket`.  
• **Symptom — High volume during package push** — expected; annotate `managed_package_deploy` suppression with vendor ticket.  
• **Symptom — DelegateUser blank** — field extraction regressions; rerun `spath`/`fieldsummary`, open TA Issue.  
• **Symptom — Alert excludes legitimate hybrid actions** — `insertPermission` may log differently across editions; widen `Action IN` clause cautiously after admin workshop.  
• **Symptom — Analyst confusion on toxic vs benign labels** — improve dictionary overlays in dashboard captions linking to RACI glossary.  
• **Symptom — Search skips events** — cron skew vs poll delay; widen window by +15m.
grandmaExplanation: We alert when someone is handed keys that let them change everything, peek at every record, or add new bosses to the customer system outside normal upkeep times, because that is how insiders or careless helpers open the door to serious harm.
